milestones: Queried pg_stat_statements to examine query execution statistics such as calls, total_time, mean_time, or rows for identifying slow or frequent queries
Queried pg_locks or related views to assess whether lock contention is occurring among concurrent transactions
Queried pg_stat_user_indexes and/or pg_indexes to evaluate index usage statistics and identify potentially redundant or unused indexes
Queried vacuum-related statistics from pg_stat_all_tables, pg_stat_user_tables, or pg_stat_progress_vacuum to assess dead tuple accumulation and vacuum activity
Analyzed query patterns or table statistics to distinguish between large data insertion issues and large data fetching issues as potential root causes
Final answer selects exactly 3 root causes from the permitted set of INSERT_LARGE_DATA, LOCK_CONTENTION, VACUUM, REDUNDANT_INDEX, FETCH_LARGE_DATA
